questions.question: To help reduce the price of oil, new supplies are needed. However, objectors oppose exploration of new sites because of the environmental damage it may cause. Why is this statement an example of the basic economic problem? A Oil is a limited resource., B Oil is expensive., C The exploration involves demand and supply., D There are external costs involved in production.
questions.text: To help reduce the price of oil, new supplies are needed. However, objectors oppose exploration of new sites because of the environmental damage it may cause. Why is this statement an example of the basic economic problem? A Oil is a limited resource., B Oil is expensive., C The exploration involves demand and supply., D There are external costs involved in production.
options: A Oil is a limited resource.
B Oil is expensive.
C The exploration involves demand and supply.
D There are external costs involved in production.

questions.question: What is an opportunity cost for a consumer spending money on a holiday? A the potential interest earned if the holiday money had been saved, B the price paid for an air ticket to the holiday destination, C the rubbish created during the holiday which affects the local community, D the satisfaction that the consumer gains from the holiday
questions.text: What is an opportunity cost for a consumer spending money on a holiday? A the potential interest earned if the holiday money had been saved, B the price paid for an air ticket to the holiday destination, C the rubbish created during the holiday which affects the local community, D the satisfaction that the consumer gains from the holiday
options: A the potential interest earned if the holiday money had been saved
B the price paid for an air ticket to the holiday destination
C the rubbish created during the holiday which affects the local community
D the satisfaction that the consumer gains from the holiday

questions.question: Helium is a gas that is limited in supply because it takes thousands of years to form. The US government holds 35% of the world’s supply of helium and has been selling its stocks. Helium is essential in medical scanners. It is also used for party balloons, which is a wasteful alternative use of a valuable good. Which two concepts apply to this statement? A demand and supply, government subsidy, B excess demand, resource allocation, C opportunity cost, private monopoly, D public sector, factors of production
questions.text: Helium is a gas that is limited in supply because it takes thousands of years to form. The US government holds 35% of the world’s supply of helium and has been selling its stocks. Helium is essential in medical scanners. It is also used for party balloons, which is a wasteful alternative use of a valuable good. Which two concepts apply to this statement? A demand and supply, government subsidy, B excess demand, resource allocation, C opportunity cost, private monopoly, D public sector, factors of production
options: A demand and supply, government subsidy
B excess demand, resource allocation
C opportunity cost, private monopoly
D public sector, factors of production

questions.question: Which combination of characteristics describes a public good? A excludable and non-rival in consumption, B excludable and rival in consumption, C non-excludable and non-rival in consumption, D non-excludable and rival in consumption

questions.question: What is an advantage of a market economy? A Equilibrium market price clears the market., B Producers and consumers have the same aims., C The government has no need to intervene in markets., D The lowest price possible is always charged. 3

questions.question: How is the rate of inflation measured? A by calculating the change in the price of goods and services from one year to the next, B by calculating the real value of all output of goods and services in an economy, C by calculating the total number of people willing and able to work but cannot find work, D by calculating the total value of exports minus the total value of imports Due to an issue with Question 22, the question has been removed from the question paper.

A by calculating the change in the price of goods and services from one year to the next, B by calculating the real value of all output of goods and services in an economy, C by calculating the total number of people willing and able to work but cannot find work, D by calculating the total value of exports minus the total value of imports Due to an issue with Question 22, the question has been removed from the question paper.
options: A by calculating the change in the price of goods and services from one year to the next
B by calculating the real value of all output of goods and services in an economy
C by calculating the total number of people willing and able to work but cannot find work
D by calculating the total value of exports minus the total value of imports Due to an issue with Question 22, the question has been removed from the question paper.

questions.question: A country specialises in the production of steel, toys and textiles. What is a disadvantage of specialisation for the country’s workers? A They cannot afford to buy products from other countries., B They could become structurally unemployed if global demand changes., C They have to learn a variety of skills., D They have too much choice which wastes their time.
questions.text: A country specialises in the production of steel, toys and textiles. What is a disadvantage of specialisation for the country’s workers? A They cannot afford to buy products from other countries., B They could become structurally unemployed if global demand changes., C They have to learn a variety of skills., D They have too much choice which wastes their time.
options: A They cannot afford to buy products from other countries.
B They could become structurally unemployed if global demand changes.
C They have to learn a variety of skills.
D They have too much choice which wastes their time.

questions.question: The US imposed tariffs on cars and motorcycles from Germany. What is the effect of these tariffs? A decrease in inflation in the US, B decrease in trade between the US and Germany, C increase in profits of car and motorcycle producers from Germany, D increase in standards of living in the US and in Germany
questions.text: The US imposed tariffs on cars and motorcycles from Germany. What is the effect of these tariffs? A decrease in inflation in the US, B decrease in trade between the US and Germany, C increase in profits of car and motorcycle producers from Germany, D increase in standards of living in the US and in Germany
options: A decrease in inflation in the US
B decrease in trade between the US and Germany
C increase in profits of car and motorcycle producers from Germany
D increase in standards of living in the US and in Germany
